title: Delivery OS — Unified Platform Health report
description: The ONE response shape every app's GET /api/health/platform (or equivalent) emits, so the Infrastructure Platform can poll every app identically. Each app aggregates its own subsystem probes (DB reachability, the drain/heartbeat liveness, discovery, event cursor, …) into this shape. The verdict is the worst-wins fold of the per-subsystem statuses (computeVerdict in platform-health.mjs); a CRITICAL subsystem that is down or unknown takes the whole platform down (fail-closed). Validate any instance with `node infra/platform-health.mjs validate`.

service.description: Stable service id (matches the config-registry service).

verdict.description: The overall verdict the platform polls on. ok = serve · degraded = serve + alarm · down = fail-closed (the route answers HTTP 503).

checkedAt.description: ISO-8601 instant the aggregate was computed.

subsystems.description: One entry per probed subsystem. A failed/timed-out probe MUST appear here as status=down with an actionable cause — never be silently omitted.

name.description: Subsystem id, e.g. database | heartbeat | event-cursor | discovery | admin-handoff.

status.description: ok = healthy · degraded = impaired · down = failed/unreachable · unknown = could not determine (never silently treated as ok).

critical.description: Does a failure here take the whole platform down? (default true).

actionable.description: The next action when not ok (optional). Config-class causes delegate to config-doctor.
